ogTitle: Stony Creek vs Stotts Creek: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Stony Creek and Stotts Creek.
heading: Stony Creek vs Stotts Creek — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Stony Creek, New South Wales and Stotts Creek, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Stony Creek has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Stotts Creek (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Stony Creek or Stotts Creek safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Stony Creek has a flood risk score of 28/100, while Stotts Creek scores 58/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Stony Creek scores 25/100 for bushfire risk. Stotts Creek scores 64/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Stony Creek or Stotts Creek?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Stony Creek has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.93% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
